content_en:
The skilled doctor uses the easiest treatment and cure first, then the more difficult or powerful medications. Therefore, the doctor moves from the less powerful to the more powerful, unless he fears that the strength of the body will be drained if he does not use the most powerful medicine first. In addition, the skilled doctor does not keep using the same medications in a way that will allow the body to get used to the medicine and thus it looses its effectiveness. We In addition mentioned that the doctor should first consider prescribing a diet before using medications. If the doctor is unsure about the nature of the disease, he should not prescribe medication before being sure of the correctness of his diagnosis. There is no harm if the doctor administers a medication that does not cause side effects or negative repercussions.

When several diseases attack the body, the doctor starts with the medicine that satisfies three condition: treating the disease that might lead to curing the other diseases, such as when confronted with a swelling and an ulcer, he starts with the swelling.

The second condition, the doctor starts with the disease that has caused the other. For example, if the Goctor is confronted with an embolism (the obstruction of an artery by an embolus, usually a piece of clotted blood which breaks away from one part of the circulatory, system and travels to another) and septic-related fever, he

—— fhe Prophetic Medicine

starts with the cause of the first disease.

Third, when one of the two diseases is more serious than the other disease, then the doctor should start with the acute ailment. Yet, the doctor should In addiuon examine the progress of the other disease.

In addition, when a disease and a symptomatic ailment both attack the body, the doctor starts with the disease, unless the symptomatic condition is stronger, such as in the case of painful constipation. The doctor first treats the pai. then treats the embolism. If the doctor can substitute excretion or extraction (of the septic substances) with hunger, fasting, sleep, then he should do so.

The Prophet's guidance _on preventing contagious diseases and enforcing quarantine

Muslim narrated that Jabir bin Abdullah said:

"Thagqifs delegation included a man with leprosy. The Prophet b sent to him, 'Go back, for we have accepted your pledge of allegiance." [Ibn Majah, Ahmad, Tbn Khuzaymah and ibn jarir}.

AI-Bokhary narrated that the Prophet if said:

"Do not keep looking at the person afflicted by leprosy." [Ahma4, At-Tayalisi, At-Tabarani and Al-

The Prophetic Medicine Kayhaqi].

Further, in the Sahihain it is narrated that the Prophet s& said:

"A healthy man should not be brought near a sick person." [Abu Dawood, Ahmad, Ibn Majah, Ahmad and AI-Bayhaqy]

Leprosy is an especially harmful disease that occurs due to the accumulation of black bile in the entire body, altering the condition or temperament and shape of the organs. In the final stages, leprosy might cause such bad corrosion that the affected limbs would start to fall off and disintegrate. Leprosy is In addition called the diseases of the lion, for three reasons. First, this disease usually attacks lions (or wild beasts). Second, because this disease causes the face to frown, like the lion's face. The third, leprosy is contagious and will devour its victim just as the lion devours its prey.

Leprosy is a contagious disease, and those who come near the person afflicted with leprosy will be bothered by the foul odor, just like those suffering from tuberculosis.

The Prophet, who was full of mercy and pity for his nation, commanded them not to expose themselves to what might bring harm to their bodies and hearts. There is

~-— The Prophetic Medicine

no doubt that certain bodies are disecse prone and are easily affected by the surrounding environment, and as we have stated, leprosy is a contagious disease. Sometimes, someone's fear of the disease might actually help the disease attack the body, because fear prepares the organs of the body for the possibility of catching the disease. Sometimes, when one even smells the foul odor emitted by persons inflicted by certain diseases, they in turn catch the same disease, if their body is susceptible to catch the disease. Some people stated that the Ahadeeth we mentioned in this chapter are overruled by some other Ahadeeth. They brought as evidence what the Prophet m, said: "There is no contagion nor evil omen." [AI-Bokhary, Moslem and Abu Dawood].

We should state that there is no contradiction between the authentic Ahadeeth, When there is an appearance of contradiction, one of the Ahadeeth might not be an authentic Hadith, sometimes, the narrators of Ahadeeth might have made mistakes, although they are truthful themselves. Or, one of the seemingly contradicted Ahadeeth might be overruling the other. Further, there are no two authentic Ahadeeth that are direct in meaning which contradict each other in every respect. Allah forbids that his truthful Messenger should ever contradict himself, and therefore, everything the Prophet b uttered is the ultimate truth, no doubt. Problems and errors sometime arise from the narrators of the Hadith, the

shortcoming in comprehending the true meaning of some Ahadeeth or the inability to distinguish between authentic and forged Ahadeeth.

Ibn Qutaibah said in his book, AI-Hadith, while talking about the enemies of Hadith, They say, "These are Ahadeeth that contradict each other. You narrated that the Prophet said, There is neither contagiousness nor evil omen.’ And that the Prophet b was told, 'Wet mange attacks the camel's lip and then the camels catch leprosy,' and that he said, 'What has transferred the ailment to the first camel?’ You In addition narrated, 'No healthy person should be brought near a sick person, and avoid the one with leprosy just as you avoid the lion.’ You In addition reported that a man with leprosy came to give his allegiance to the Prophet as on Islam and that he said to him that his pledge is accepted, ordering him to return without meeting him. He In addition said, "Evil omen is in three things: the woman, the animal and the house,' and all these statements contradict each other."

Abu Muhammad (Ibn Qutaibah) then commented, "We say that there is no contradiction, because each statement has its own meaning and timeframe. When the Hadith is used in its proper context, the appearance of contradiction will not be substantiated. There are two types of contagion, one of them is leprosy like contagion. For instance, when the odor of the person afflicted with

——— The Prophetic Medicine

leprosy is felt by those who talk and sit near to the leper for a long time, they might fall victim to the same disease. Such is the case with the wife of the person with leprosy who remains under the same roof with him and thus catches the disease. In addition, the children of the person afflicted with leprosy might catch the disease later on in their life (hereditary). Such is the case with those suffering from tuberculosis, hectic fever and mange. The doctors order that those suffering from tuberculosis and leprosy should be avoided, not because they fear that such cases are contagious but because the bad odor that emits from such people might affect the healthy persons when exposed to it for a long time. The doctors are the farthest people from believing in evil or good omens. The same argument is true in the case with the Nugbah, which is a wet type of leprosy or mange that attacks camels. When the infected came mixes or mingles with other healthy camels, the infection will be transferred through the water [open sores] or the mucus that the camel produces. This is the meaning that the Prophet b intended when he said:

’No sick person should be brought near a healthy person.’

He disliked that the sick people associated with healthy people, so that the healthy are not exposed to any substances or rashes that the body of the sick person produces. The second type of contagion, is the plague, for

instance, that appears in a land and the person thus seeks to flee that land for fear of catching the disease. The Prophet s& said, ‘If it (the Plague) appears in a land where you are residing, do not depart that land. If it appears in a land, do not enter it,’ The Prophet gs ordered the people not to depart the land afflicted by the plague, as they might be thinking that by fleecing they would be avoiding Allah's appointed destiny. In addition, the Prophet “ ordered the people not to enter a land afflicted by the plague, for remaining in the healthy land brings calmness to their hearts and tranquility in their livelihood. In addition, this type includes the husband who suffers an affliction and attributes it to the bad omen of his wife or the house as is common. This is the type that the Prophet b meant when he said, "There is neither contagion...’ "

Some other people said the Prophet safe only recommended that leprosy patients be avoided and that it is still allowed to eat with them, as the Prophet himself did.

Some other people said that these Ahadeeth contain specific commands for different types of people. For instance, some people have such a strong faith and reliance on Allah that the strength of their faith would save them from contagious diseases, just as the natural powers that exist in the body sometimes fend off the harm of the disease. Sune people, on the other hand, do not

——— The Prophetic Medicine

have a strong faith, so the Prophet * commanded them to be cautious, The Prophet b implemented his own commands in both cases, so that the strong imitate his acts, depending on their trust in Allah. On the other hand, the weak would imitate the Prophet b (when he avoided leprous patients, for instance) to be cautions. Both ways are correct, but one is suitable for those who have strong faith while the other is suitable for those who have weak faith. Consequently, both groups of the believers will have their own method that is suitable for their own situation. As another example, the Prophet s has used fire for medicinal purposes, cauterization. Yet, he praised whoever refrains from using cauterization and meationed avoiding it with the good deeds of shunning the bad omen and having total dependence on Allah. There are many other examples on this subject. The Prophet's method is always very gentle, and those who comprehend it in the correct manner will get rid of many suspicions that the authentic Sunnah contradicts itself.

Some other people stated that ordering the people to run away from the one with leprosy prevents the transfer of the disease by mingling with the sick or by smelling their odor when such mingling and associations are extensive. As for short visitations with such people for a good purpose, there is no harm in it and catching the disease will not be plausible during these brief contacts. The Prophet b prohibited long exposures to sick people to
content_id:
Dokter yang terampil menggunakan pengobatan dan penyembuhan yang paling mudah terlebih dahulu, kemudian obat-obatan yang lebih sulit atau lebih kuat. Oleh karena itu, dokter bergerak dari yang kurang kuat ke yang lebih kuat, kecuali jika ia khawatir kekuatan tubuh akan terkuras habis apabila tidak menggunakan obat yang paling kuat terlebih dahulu. Selain itu, dokter yang terampil tidak terus-menerus menggunakan obat yang sama dengan cara yang akan membuat tubuh terbiasa dengan obat tersebut sehingga kehilangan efektivitasnya. Kami juga telah menyebutkan bahwa dokter sebaiknya mempertimbangkan untuk meresepkan diet terlebih dahulu sebelum menggunakan obat-obatan. Jika dokter tidak yakin tentang sifat penyakitnya, ia tidak boleh meresepkan obat sebelum yakin akan kebenaran diagnosisnya. Tidak ada salahnya jika dokter memberikan obat yang tidak menimbulkan efek samping atau dampak negatif.

Ketika beberapa penyakit menyerang tubuh, dokter memulai dengan obat yang memenuhi tiga syarat: mengobati penyakit yang mungkin mengarah pada penyembuhan penyakit lainnya, seperti ketika menghadapi pembengkakan dan bisul, ia memulai dengan pembengkakan.

Syarat kedua, dokter memulai dengan penyakit yang menjadi penyebab penyakit lainnya. Misalnya, jika dokter menghadapi emboli (penyumbatan arteri oleh embolus, biasanya berupa gumpalan darah yang terlepas dari satu bagian sistem peredaran darah dan berpindah ke bagian lain) dan demam terkait septik, ia

—— Pengobatan Nabawi

memulai dengan penyebab penyakit pertama.

Ketiga, ketika salah satu dari dua penyakit lebih serius daripada penyakit lainnya, maka dokter harus memulai dengan penyakit yang akut. Namun, dokter juga harus memperhatikan perkembangan penyakit yang lain.

Selain itu, ketika suatu penyakit dan gejala penyerta keduanya menyerang tubuh, dokter memulai dengan penyakitnya, kecuali jika kondisi gejalanya lebih kuat, seperti dalam kasus sembelit yang menyakitkan. Dokter terlebih dahulu mengobati rasa sakitnya, kemudian mengobati emboli. Jika dokter dapat menggantikan pengeluaran atau ekstraksi (zat-zat septik) dengan lapar, puasa, atau tidur, maka ia sebaiknya melakukannya.

Petunjuk Nabi ﷺ tentang pencegahan penyakit menular dan penerapan karantina

Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa Jabir bin Abdullah berkata:

"Dalam delegasi Tsaqif terdapat seorang pria yang menderita kusta. Nabi ﷺ mengirim pesan kepadanya, 'Kembalilah, karena kami telah menerima baiatmu.'" [Ibn Majah, Ahmad, Ibn Khuzaymah dan Ibn Jarir].

Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Janganlah terus-menerus memandang orang yang terkena kusta." [Ahmad, Ath-Thayalisi, Ath-Thabrani dan Al-

Pengobatan Nabawi Bayhaqi].

Lebih lanjut, dalam Shahihain diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Janganlah orang yang sehat didekatkan kepada orang yang sakit." [Abu Dawud, Ahmad, Ibn Majah, Ahmad dan Al-Bayhaqi]

Kusta adalah penyakit yang sangat berbahaya yang terjadi akibat penumpukan empedu hitam di seluruh tubuh, mengubah kondisi atau temperamen dan bentuk organ-organ tubuh. Pada tahap akhir, kusta dapat menyebabkan korosi yang begitu parah sehingga anggota tubuh yang terkena mulai rontok dan hancur. Kusta juga disebut penyakit singa, karena tiga alasan. Pertama, penyakit ini biasanya menyerang singa (atau binatang buas). Kedua, karena penyakit ini menyebabkan wajah berkerut, seperti wajah singa. Ketiga, kusta bersifat menular dan akan memangsa korbannya sebagaimana singa memangsa mangsanya.

Kusta adalah penyakit menular, dan mereka yang berada di dekat orang yang terkena kusta akan terganggu oleh bau busuknya, sama seperti mereka yang menderita tuberkulosis.

Nabi ﷺ, yang penuh dengan rahmat dan kasih sayang terhadap umatnya, memerintahkan mereka untuk tidak membiarkan diri mereka terpapar pada hal-hal yang dapat membahayakan tubuh dan hati mereka. Tidak

—— Pengobatan Nabawi

diragukan lagi bahwa tubuh tertentu rentan terhadap penyakit dan mudah terpengaruh oleh lingkungan sekitar, dan sebagaimana telah kami sebutkan, kusta adalah penyakit menular. Terkadang, ketakutan seseorang terhadap penyakit justru dapat membantu penyakit tersebut menyerang tubuh, karena rasa takut mempersiapkan organ-organ tubuh untuk kemungkinan tertular penyakit. Terkadang, ketika seseorang bahkan mencium bau busuk yang dikeluarkan oleh orang-orang yang terkena penyakit tertentu, mereka pun tertular penyakit yang sama, jika tubuh mereka rentan terhadapnya. Sebagian orang menyatakan bahwa hadits-hadits yang kami sebutkan dalam bab ini dinasakh oleh hadits-hadits lain. Mereka membawa sebagai dalil apa yang Nabi ﷺ sabdakan: "Tidak ada penularan dan tidak ada pertanda buruk." [Al-Bukhari, Muslim dan Abu Dawud].

Kami harus menyatakan bahwa tidak ada kontradiksi di antara hadits-hadits yang shahih. Ketika tampak adanya kontradiksi, salah satu hadits mungkin bukan hadits yang shahih, terkadang para perawi hadits mungkin melakukan kesalahan, meskipun mereka sendiri adalah orang-orang yang jujur. Atau, salah satu hadits yang tampak bertentangan mungkin menasakh yang lain. Lebih jauh, tidak ada dua hadits shahih yang jelas maknanya yang saling bertentangan dalam segala hal. Allah melarang bahwa Rasul-Nya yang jujur pernah bertentangan dengan dirinya sendiri, dan oleh karena itu, segala sesuatu yang Nabi ﷺ ucapkan adalah kebenaran mutlak, tanpa keraguan. Masalah dan kesalahan terkadang muncul dari para perawi hadits,

kekurangan dalam memahami makna sejati dari sebagian hadits atau ketidakmampuan membedakan antara hadits yang shahih dan hadits yang palsu.

Ibn Qutaibah berkata dalam bukunya, Al-Hadits, ketika membahas tentang musuh-musuh hadits, Mereka berkata, "Ini adalah hadits-hadits yang saling bertentangan. Kalian meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda, 'Tidak ada penularan dan tidak ada pertanda buruk.' Dan bahwa Nabi ﷺ diberitahu, 'Kudis basah menyerang bibir unta kemudian unta-unta lainnya tertular kusta,' dan bahwa beliau bersabda, 'Siapa yang menularkan penyakit kepada unta yang pertama?' Kalian juga meriwayatkan, 'Janganlah orang yang sehat didekatkan kepada orang yang sakit, dan hindarilah orang yang terkena kusta sebagaimana kalian menghindari singa.' Kalian juga melaporkan bahwa seorang pria dengan kusta datang untuk memberikan baiatnya kepada Nabi ﷺ untuk masuk Islam dan bahwa beliau berkata kepadanya bahwa baiatnya diterima, memerintahkannya untuk kembali tanpa menemuinya. Beliau juga bersabda, 'Pertanda buruk ada pada tiga hal: wanita, hewan dan rumah,' dan semua pernyataan ini saling bertentangan."

Abu Muhammad (Ibn Qutaibah) kemudian berkomentar, "Kami katakan bahwa tidak ada kontradiksi, karena setiap pernyataan memiliki makna dan konteks waktunya sendiri. Ketika hadits digunakan dalam konteks yang tepat, tampaknya kontradiksi tidak akan terbukti. Ada dua jenis penularan, salah satunya adalah penularan seperti kusta. Misalnya, ketika bau orang yang terkena

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

kusta tercium oleh mereka yang berbicara dan duduk di dekat penderita kusta dalam waktu yang lama, mereka mungkin menjadi korban penyakit yang sama. Demikian pula halnya dengan istri penderita kusta yang tetap tinggal di bawah atap yang sama dengannya sehingga tertular penyakit tersebut. Selain itu, anak-anak penderita kusta mungkin tertular penyakit tersebut di kemudian hari (keturunan). Demikian pula halnya dengan mereka yang menderita tuberkulosis, demam hektik dan kudis. Para dokter memerintahkan agar mereka yang menderita tuberkulosis dan kusta dihindari, bukan karena mereka takut bahwa kasus-kasus tersebut menular tetapi karena bau busuk yang keluar dari orang-orang tersebut dapat mempengaruhi orang-orang sehat ketika terpapar dalam waktu lama. Para dokter adalah orang-orang yang paling jauh dari mempercayai pertanda buruk atau baik. Argumen yang sama berlaku dalam kasus Nugbah, yaitu jenis kusta basah atau kudis yang menyerang unta. Ketika unta yang terinfeksi bercampur atau bergaul dengan unta-unta sehat lainnya, infeksi akan berpindah melalui air [luka terbuka] atau lendir yang dihasilkan unta tersebut. Inilah makna yang dimaksud oleh Nabi ﷺ ketika beliau bersabda:

'Janganlah orang yang sakit didekatkan kepada orang yang sehat.'

Beliau tidak menyukai orang-orang yang sakit bergaul dengan orang-orang sehat, agar orang-orang sehat tidak terpapar zat-zat atau ruam yang dihasilkan oleh tubuh orang yang sakit. Jenis penularan yang kedua adalah wabah, misalnya,

yang muncul di suatu negeri dan seseorang berusaha melarikan diri dari negeri itu karena takut tertular penyakit. Nabi ﷺ bersabda, 'Jika wabah (tha'un) muncul di suatu negeri tempat kalian tinggal, janganlah meninggalkan negeri itu. Jika wabah muncul di suatu negeri, janganlah memasukinya.' Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan orang-orang untuk tidak meninggalkan negeri yang terkena wabah, karena mereka mungkin berpikir bahwa dengan melarikan diri mereka akan menghindari takdir yang telah Allah tetapkan. Selain itu, Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan orang-orang untuk tidak memasuki negeri yang terkena wabah, karena tinggal di negeri yang sehat membawa ketenangan bagi hati mereka dan ketenteraman dalam kehidupan mereka. Selain itu, jenis ini termasuk suami yang mengalami musibah dan mengaitkannya dengan pertanda buruk dari istrinya atau rumahnya sebagaimana yang umum terjadi. Inilah jenis yang dimaksud oleh Nabi ﷺ ketika beliau bersabda, 'Tidak ada penularan...'"

Sebagian orang lain mengatakan bahwa Nabi ﷺ hanya menganjurkan agar penderita kusta dijauhi, dan bahwa masih diperbolehkan makan bersama mereka, sebagaimana Nabi sendiri pernah melakukannya.

Sebagian orang lain mengatakan bahwa hadith-hadith ini mengandung perintah khusus untuk tipe orang yang berbeda. Misalnya, sebagian orang memiliki iman dan tawakkul kepada Allah yang sangat kuat sehingga kekuatan iman mereka akan melindungi mereka dari penyakit menular, sebagaimana kekuatan alami yang ada dalam tubuh terkadang menangkal bahaya penyakit. Sebagian orang lain, di sisi lain, tidak

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

memiliki iman yang kuat, sehingga Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan mereka untuk berhati-hati. Nabi ﷺ menerapkan perintahnya sendiri dalam kedua keadaan tersebut, agar orang yang kuat meniru perbuatan beliau dengan bergantung pada tawakkul mereka kepada Allah. Di sisi lain, orang yang lemah akan meniru Nabi ﷺ (ketika beliau menjauhi penderita kusta, misalnya) untuk berhati-hati. Kedua cara tersebut benar, tetapi yang satu cocok bagi mereka yang memiliki iman kuat sementara yang lain cocok bagi mereka yang memiliki iman lemah. Dengan demikian, kedua kelompok orang beriman akan memiliki metode masing-masing yang sesuai dengan keadaan mereka. Sebagai contoh lain, Nabi ﷺ pernah menggunakan api untuk tujuan pengobatan, yaitu kay (kauterisasi). Namun beliau memuji siapa saja yang menahan diri dari menggunakan kauterisasi dan menyebutkan menghindarinya bersama amal-amal baik seperti menjauhi anggapan sial dan memiliki tawakkul sepenuhnya kepada Allah. Ada banyak contoh lain mengenai masalah ini. Metode Nabi selalu sangat lembut, dan mereka yang memahaminya dengan cara yang benar akan terbebas dari banyak keraguan bahwa Sunnah yang sahih saling bertentangan.

Sebagian orang lain menyatakan bahwa perintah untuk menjauhi penderita kusta adalah untuk mencegah penularan penyakit melalui pergaulan dengan orang sakit atau melalui bau mereka ketika pergaulan dan hubungan tersebut berlangsung lama. Adapun kunjungan singkat kepada mereka untuk tujuan yang baik, maka tidak ada salahnya dan tertular penyakit tidak akan mungkin terjadi selama kontak singkat tersebut. Nabi ﷺ melarang paparan yang lama terhadap orang sakit untuk

Therefore, the doctor moves from the less powerful to the more powerful, unless he fears that the strength of the body will be drained if he does not use the most powerful medicine first. In addition, the skilled doctor does not keep using the same medications in a way that will allow the body to get used to the medicine and thus it looses its effectiveness. There is no harm if the doctor administers a medication that does not cause side effects or negative repercussions.

The second condition, the doctor starts with the disease that has caused the other. —— fhe Prophetic Medicine

starts with the cause of the first disease.

Third, when one of the two diseases is more serious than the other disease, then the doctor should start with the acute ailment. Yet, the doctor should In addiuon examine the progress of the other disease.

In addition, when a disease and a symptomatic ailment both attack the body, the doctor starts with the disease, unless the symptomatic condition is stronger, such as in the case of painful constipation. The doctor first treats the pai. then treats the embolism. If the doctor can substitute excretion or extraction (of the septic substances) with hunger, fasting, sleep, then he should do so.

no doubt that certain bodies are disecse prone and are easily affected by the surrounding environment, and as we have stated, leprosy is a contagious disease. [AI-Bokhary, Moslem and Abu Dawood].

We should state that there is no contradiction between the authentic Ahadeeth, When there is an appearance of contradiction, one of the Ahadeeth might not be an authentic الحديث, sometimes, the narrators of Ahadeeth might have made mistakes, although they are truthful themselves. Or, one of the seemingly contradicted Ahadeeth might be overruling the other. Further, there are no two authentic Ahadeeth that are direct in meaning which contradict each other in every respect. الله forbids that his truthful Messenger should ever contradict himself, and therefore, everything النبي ﷺ b uttered is the ultimate truth, no doubt. Problems and errors sometime arise from the narrators of the الحديث, the

shortcoming in comprehending the true meaning of some Ahadeeth or the inability to distinguish between authentic and forged Ahadeeth.

Ibn Qutaibah said in his book, AI-الحديث, while talking about the enemies of الحديث, They say, "These are Ahadeeth that contradict each other. You narrated that النبي ﷺ said, There is neither contagiousness nor evil omen.’ And that النبي ﷺ b was told, 'Wet mange attacks the camel's lip and then the camels catch leprosy,' and that he said, 'What has transferred the ailment to the first camel?’ You In addition narrated, 'No healthy person should be brought near a sick person, and avoid the one with leprosy just as you avoid the lion.’ You In addition reported that a man with leprosy came to give his allegiance to النبي ﷺ as on Islam and that he said to him that his pledge is accepted, ordering him to return without meeting him. He In addition said, "Evil omen is in three things: the woman, the animal and the house,' and all these statements contradict each other."

Abu Muhammad (Ibn Qutaibah) then commented, "We say that there is no contradiction, because each statement has its own meaning and timeframe. When the الحديث is used in its proper context, the appearance of contradiction will not be substantiated. There are two types of contagion, one of them is leprosy like contagion. For instance, when the odor of the person afflicted with

——— The Prophetic Medicine

leprosy is felt by those who talk and sit near to the leper for a long time, they might fall victim to the same disease. Such is the case with the wife of the person with leprosy who remains under the same roof with him and thus catches the disease. In addition, the children of the person afflicted with leprosy might catch the disease later on in their life (hereditary). Such is the case with those suffering from tuberculosis, hectic fever and mange. The doctors order that those suffering from tuberculosis and leprosy should be avoided, not because they fear that such cases are contagious but because the bad odor that emits from such people might affect the healthy persons when exposed to it for a long time. The doctors are the farthest people from believing in evil or good omens. The same argument is true in the case with the Nugbah, which is a wet type of leprosy or mange that attacks camels. When the infected came mixes or mingles with other healthy camels, the infection will be transferred through the water [open sores] or the mucus that the camel produces. This is the meaning that النبي ﷺ b intended when he said:

’No sick person should be brought near a healthy person.’

He disliked that the sick people associated with healthy people, so that the healthy are not exposed to any substances or rashes that the body of the sick person produces. The second type of contagion, is the plague, for

instance, that appears in a land and the person thus seeks to flee that land for fear of catching the disease. النبي ﷺ s& said, ‘If it (the Plague) appears in a land where you are residing, do not depart that land. If it appears in a land, do not enter it,’ النبي ﷺ gs ordered the people not to depart the land afflicted by the plague, as they might be thinking that by fleecing they would be avoiding الله's appointed destiny. In addition, the Prophet “ ordered the people not to enter a land afflicted by the plague, for remaining in the healthy land brings calmness to their hearts and tranquility in their livelihood. In addition, this type includes the husband who suffers an affliction and attributes it to the bad omen of his wife or the house as is common. This is the type that النبي ﷺ b meant when he said, "There is neither contagion...’ "

Some other people said النبي ﷺ safe only recommended that leprosy patients be avoided and that it is still allowed to eat with them, as النبي ﷺ himself did.

Some other people said that these Ahadeeth contain specific commands for different types of people. For instance, some people have such a strong faith and reliance on الله that the strength of their faith would save them from contagious diseases, just as the natural powers that exist in the body sometimes fend off the harm of the disease. Sune people, on the other hand, do not

——— The Prophetic Medicine

have a strong faith, so النبي ﷺ * commanded them to be cautious, النبي ﷺ b implemented his own commands in both cases, so that the strong imitate his acts, depending on their trust in الله. On the other hand, the weak would imitate النبي ﷺ b (when he avoided leprous patients, for instance) to be cautions. Both ways are correct, but one is suitable for those who have strong faith while the other is suitable for those who have weak faith. Consequently, both groups of the believers will have their own method that is suitable for their own situation. As another example, النبي ﷺ s has used fire for medicinal purposes, cauterization. Yet, he praised whoever refrains from using cauterization and meationed avoiding it with the good deeds of shunning the bad omen and having total dependence on الله. There are many other examples on this subject. النبي ﷺ's method is always very gentle, and those who comprehend it in the correct manner will get rid of many suspicions that the authentic السنة النبوية contradicts itself.

Some other people stated that ordering the people to run away from the one with leprosy prevents the transfer of the disease by mingling with the sick or by smelling their odor when such mingling and associations are extensive. As for short visitations with such people for a good purpose, there is no harm in it and catching the disease will not be plausible during these brief contacts. النبي ﷺ b prohibited long exposures to sick people to
